content:
7 min read
Just now

--

Second Brain is a Note taking app as usual , you can write your notes , u can delete that note , but suppose what if we have 100+ notes and suddenly we get a thought of one note in whcih we had stored something of our past , something which we want to share with someone .
But now u got 100 notes to search among them so im building a application where you write the note and save it but The best part of our application is You can ask AI for that specific information its like built in Ginnie on your notes.So lets talk about this further.
Architecture
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Problems I faced During This Project
Building of Notes part is not very scary part , i have done some projects with MERN stack so i build the note part with MERN but at this time I added some twist ,I used type script over regular Java Script — Why .. So type script just provides a security layer , it preovides u boundaries to working on , it tells u that u are wrong here .. so that error does not come when u run the project .. let me show you example of this .
Press enter or click to view image in full size

So here u can see that i had declared a INote interface now this interface loos all over the notes ,whenever i use something from the Interface it checks for the used data type of any error and if there is an error he simply puts a red line .So these All the benefits of Type Script over regular java script , although there is few Syntax differnce among TypeScript and JavaScript but most of the time they seem Similar.
If You want One liner for Type Script this can be your one liner — It catches a whole class of bugs before the code runs, gives better autocomplete, and documents what my data looks like — which matters more and more as a project grows.
So Building NOTE part is not that big of deal .The Real Problem comes with the RAG system ,i never touched rag — i never builded anything with RAG ,but thats fun part also .So lets go in RAG
RAG-(Retrieve ,Augment ,Generate)
What i got to understand that RAG is 3 step process and his name also suggests that ,but before RAG i think we need to talk about LLM — LArge Langauge Model, becuase somehow i think RAG works because of LLM ,so LLM are very efficient very smart models ,companies build LLM ,train them on milions of data so that they can be smart.
And LLM’s dont know about my notes or anything ,so here we need to retrieve the relevant note and then we hand over the note to LLM then llm answers the question asked according to the given prompt and yes we have to pass the prompt also ,let me show you guys our RAG code.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

You can see in this i had commented all the three Steps of rag ,we retrieve the data using .find method and we need data for specific owner thats why we do req.userID and we store the upcoming result in notes .
Then we turn the recived notes into context which our LLM can understand we map it out with tiltle and content.
Then we provide a prompt to it and then we save .
And here comes last part GENERATE:
Generation of code is done by ai.models.generateContent({}), in this we pass the model name and also the prompt .And then we get a response in JSON format.
So here is my understanding on RAG part.
And here is the problem we currently have when we retrieve the Note our ai system jsut dump all the notes to the prompt and i think this can work for like when we have small number of notes but this will not work when we will have very large number of notes ,so what we do now …..now we will turnseach note into a list of numbers that captures its meaning.by Using embedding.
Embedding
An embedding turns each note into a list of numbers that captures its meaning. Then, when you ask a question, instead of sending everything, you can find just the few notes whose meaning is closest to your question and send only those.
So why we need this ,im building this project keeping in mind that one day it will have millions of users- so scaling should be in mind from day 1 ,thats why embedding is important.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Embedding length: 3072
So by this code all the notes changes into array of number and they look something like this:
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Alot of numbers : they are in total 3072.
Now the thing is what we do of all these number so so this is so fasinating part now in our data base for normally we have index system which searches the database but now we can have Vector Search index in our data base so lets talk about that.
Vector Search index
A regular database index makes normal lookups fast. A vector index makes “find the closest vectors” fast — so when someone asks a question, databse can instantly find the most similar notes instead of comparing against every single one.
So now we have all our notes in numbers and our query in number so that will be very eassy to get the nearest or most similar 3 serach result and this is the whole point of Embedding.
And this part is done on from your database side so im using MOngoDb Atlas i will share the Steps of that .
Open your cluster → find the “Atlas Search” / “Search & Vector Search” tab (near “Collections”).

• Click “Create Search Index”.

• Choose Vector Search (the JSON editor option), not the regular one.

• Pick your database ………… → collection ……..

• Index name:

• Paste this as the index definition:
This is the definition im using
{
“fields”: [
{ “type”: “vector”, “path”: “embedding”, “numDimensions”: 3072, “similarity”: “cosine” },
{ “type”: “filter”, “path”: “owner” }
]
}{
“fields”: [
{ “type”: “vector”, “path”: “embedding”, “numDimensions”: 3072, “similarity”: “cosine” },
{ “type”: “filter”, “path”: “owner” }
]
}
Now our retrieve is using this Note.find({ owner: req.userId })
And this gives all notes we will change this to this-
Press enter or click to view image in full size

So this only gives the most similar search result .
ADN this concludes our RAG part with embedding and with vector index search.
Here is A summary of all RAG+EMBEDDING+VECTOR SEARCH
An LLM is smart but doesn’t know my data, so I use RAG: retrieve the user’s notes → augment the prompt with them (plus a grounding instruction that stops hallucination) → generate an answer with the LLM. The frontend is just a controlled input + a fetch to a protected /ai/chat route. The retrieve step uses embeddings + vector search (MongoDB Atlas $vectorSearch) to pull only the relevant notes by meaning, so it scales. Same three steps — a smart retrieve. The AI doesn't learn your data; you hand it the right pieces at question time.
What if Any One pushes a note from computer and one from mobile — Note Stale :A New Problem Arise
Solution we need a websocket — A WebSocket is a persistent, two-way pipe that stays open — either side can send a message any time. So the server can push “hey, a new note!” to the client the instant it happens.
We’ll use Socket.IO — a library that makes WebSockets easy (auto-reconnect, events, “rooms”).
We install two dependencies one in server and other in client .
In server -> npm install socket.io
In client -> npm install socket.io-client
But we dont need much of websocket for now — so im doing this part with AI.
So Now we are done with this project .
AND ready to Deploy It.
